title: Indicator notes — hand-curated sidecar
description: Optional sidecar for an indicator artifact, holding the small set of fields that do NOT belong inside the artifact's authoritative metadata: cross-indicator graph edges (`related[]`), a citizen-facing editorial voice (`editor_note_md`), policy-context bullets that change faster than the data itself (`policy_context[]`), and renderer chart hints (`chart_defaults`). Lives at `datasets/indicators/in/<topic>/<id>.notes.json` next to its sibling artifact `<id>.json`. Sidecar absent ⇒ corresponding sections are simply omitted from the per-indicator page. Per TODO/PER-INDICATOR-DOCS-PLAN.md §'Generation strategy — hybrid with thin seam' (Phase 4).

x-changelog.description: Initial schema. Locks the four optional fields (related, editor_note_md, policy_context, chart_defaults) plus the required `for` basename so the two-tier validator (CLAUDE.md §11) treats these files as first-class instead of orphans. All payload fields are optional — a sidecar with only `$schema`/`$schema_version`/`for` is valid (and useless, but forward-compatible for stub commits).

for.description: Basename of the sibling indicator artifact this sidecar annotates (e.g. "state_pension_expenditure_inr_crore.json"). The validator does not enforce that the sibling actually exists — that check belongs to the page generator (which silently drops orphan sidecars).

related.description: Cross-indicator graph edges. Each entry is the `id` of another indicator artifact (e.g. "economy/state_nsdp_current_inr_crore"). The page generator renders these as a bulleted list under '## Related indicators' with a hyperlink to each peer's auto-generated page. The validator does NOT enforce that the target ids resolve — orphan edges become broken links the doc-build catches.

editor_note_md.description: A short Markdown paragraph (or two) in a human voice — the one place a sentence like 'Pair this with the constant-prices sibling when single-base purity matters' belongs. Inline links allowed; headings disallowed by convention (the page generator wraps the body under a single '## Editor's note' H2).

policy_context.description: Bullet-list of citizen-facing policy / political context: the named debate, the scheme that drives the line on the chart, the political-economy frame. Distinct from the artifact's `notes` field, which is methodological. Examples: 'Old Pension Scheme restoration debate; NPS adoption dates by state', '15th FC award window: FY21 → FY26'.

chart_defaults.description: Renderer hints that tune the citizen-facing chart for this specific indicator without touching the artifact. Optional and overridable from the frontend.

preferred_facet.description: When the indicator has multiple `rows[].facet` values, pin the default-shown facet here (e.g. 'coal' for installed-capacity-by-source). null = renderer chooses.

allowed_negative.description: Whether the renderer should expect / accept negative values (e.g. external-balance can be negative; population cannot). Default false.
